title: Detection of bacteria in antibiotic-treated diatom cultures and cell harvesting
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"vu1f","text":"We illustrate a simple and rapid method for detecting the presence/absence of bacteria in diatom cultures using DAPI and fluorescence microscopy. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"asa0g","text":"We also describe how we harvest cells for genomic DNA extraction once we have assessed that bacteria are absent from the culture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e86n1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1br3i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: For the antibiotic treatment to obtain axenic cultures, see the published protocol: Axenic Diatoms cultures protocol dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgudjws6 DMI6000 b (Fully Automated Inverted Research Microscope, LEICA, https://www.leica-microsystems.com/products/light-, https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/dagw5vhn.jpg ) Transfer 1 mL of Culture in a new 2 mL tube.
Add 1,6% of neutralized formaldehyde to fix cells and mix gently by inversion
Add 1uL of DAPI 1 mg/mL and mix gently by inversion. Keep away from direct light 4,6-Diamidino-2-Phenylindole, Dihydrochloride (DAPI) Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #D1306

Transfer cells in a 35mm glass bottom dish. µ-Dish 35 mm, high Glass Bottom (A 35 mm imaging dish with a glass bottom for use in TIRF, single molecule and super-resolution microscopy applications, ibidi, https://ibidi.com/dishes/176--dish-35-mm-high-glas, https://ibidi.com/dishes/176--dish-35-mm-high-glass-bottom.html?gclid=CjwKCAjwu5CDBhB9EiwA0w6sLRCp_f1-NVcLuPmh0y2m2hKhe57pQ0Sl-TTwo4X265451aOxRI_45BoC2XsQAvD_BwE ) Incubate for 10 min away from direct light to perimt cells sedimentation

Use an inverted microscope with fluorescence excitation. * We use a Leica DMI 6000 B microscope. Set light sources: bright-field Fluorescence for chlorophyll (580 / 604 nm) Fluorescence for DAPI (364 / 454 nm)
Assess bacteria absence-presence in the cultures. Move across the plate, focusing on the cells and on the bottom of the dish.
Filter cells on MF-Millipore™ Membrane Filter, 1.2 µm pore size, gridded 47 mm.
Move the filter in a new 50 mL tube using tweezers
Rinse the filter with 1 mL of clean medium using a pasteur pipette pipet gently, to avoid cell lysis
Collect the resuspended cells in a new 15 mL tube
Pellet cells by centrifugation 15 min. 2000 g 18°C and discard supernatant
Freeze pellet immediately in liquid nitrogen
creator: Francesco Manfellotto
